commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Their poison is like the poison of a serpent: they are like the deaf adder that stops its ear; Madness; or "poison "chamath. (Menochius) Deaf asp. This is the most dangerous species. The ancients attempted to charm serpents. But these courtiers were deaf to every proof of David's innocence, (Calmet, Diss. ) and would receive no admonition, stopping their ears, like asps. (Worthington) The prophet speaks conformably to the received opinion, (Berthier) without determining it to be true. (Menochius).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
